spec_match.rationale: The cache row correctly flips from SURVIVES to cleared at Chrome 134, matching the feature's headline. But the rest of the matrix is invented timing: storage clearance at v115, broader storage at v125 — these are approximations, not actual milestone-accurate dates for state partitioning, and the page presents them as fact.

interactive.rationale: Chrome version dropdown + 3pc toggle drive a real re-render of a 6×6 matrix with red SURVIVES / green cleared cells. Five versions × two cookie modes = ten distinct states a visitor can flip through.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a 6×5 matrix showing which combinations survive vs are cleared, with v134 being the cache loophole closure; page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the matrix shape well; missing the SSO/OAuth carve-out (none of the techniques get exempted by user interaction). The 'hypothetical Chrome 140 + fp surface' is speculation that visitors might take as fact.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links the feature index, sibling demos, chromestatus.com/feature/6299570819301376, and the PrivacyCG explainer.

design_system.rationale: .survives sets background --accent-rose with `color: white` (literal); .cleared sets background --accent-emerald with `color: white`. The white-on-emerald combo at 0.78rem is borderline AA — needs a contrast check, and the literal 'white' should be `var(--bg-ivory)`.

openQuestions.title: Cite milestone-accurate version dates
openQuestions.detail: The matrix asserts storage clearance landed at v115 and broader storage at v125 — these need to be either verified against the chromestatus shipping records for bounce-tracking mitigations, or annotated as 'illustrative milestones'. Misleading dates undermine the page's authority.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'user interaction' modifier
openQuestions.detail: A tracker that gets user interaction during the bounce is exempt from mitigation. Add a third dropdown ('tracker received user interaction: yes/no') and have the survival() function exit early for all techniques when the answer is yes. That makes the SSO/OAuth carve-out part of the matrix.

openQuestions.title: Drop or annotate the 'hypothetical Chrome 140' row
openQuestions.detail: Speculating about a future fingerprinting-surface mitigation in a reference matrix risks being read as a Chrome roadmap. Either tag it visibly as 'illustrative — not on the roadmap' or remove it.

openQuestions.title: Use design-token foreground for survives/cleared cells
openQuestions.detail: Both .survives and .cleared set `color: white` literally rather than `var(--bg-ivory)`, and the contrast at 0.78rem font-mono on accent-rose / accent-emerald needs an AA audit (4.5:1 normal text). Switch to design tokens and verify.

summary: The 6×6 matrix is a strong overview concept and the cache-loophole closure at v134 is the visible headline. The chromestatus invariant is now satisfied; remaining concrete fixes are verifying the milestone dates and surfacing the user-interaction exemption. Optionally drop the hypothetical v140 row.
